hero.headline: Find real problems people complain about — and turn them into SaaS ideas.

hero.previewPain2: Manual invoice creation eats into billable time
hero.previewPain3: Lack of professional branding on invoices

invoicingFreelancers.summary: Freelancers struggle with fragmented invoicing workflows. Manual invoice creation, late payment chasing, and lack of professional branding emerge as top complaints.
invoicingFreelancers.topPainPoint: Spending hours chasing clients for late payments with no automated reminders or professional follow-up sequences.
invoicingFreelancers.suggestedProduct: A lightweight invoicing app with smart payment reminders, client-specific payment terms, and Stripe integration—built for solo freelancers, not agencies.

crmTherapists.summary: Mental health practitioners need HIPAA-compliant client management that handles intake forms and session notes without the complexity of enterprise CRMs.
crmTherapists.topPainPoint: Using spreadsheets or generic CRMs that aren't built for therapy workflows—missing intake forms, secure note-taking, and session-based client views.
crmTherapists.suggestedProduct: A HIPAA-compliant practice management tool focused on solo therapists: intake forms, encrypted session notes, appointment scheduling, and simple client progress dashboards.

schedulingTutors.summary: Tutors juggling multiple students and time zones need flexible scheduling with recurring sessions, make-up classes, and payment collection.
schedulingTutors.topPainPoint: Constantly rescheduling sessions manually, losing track of which students need make-ups, and wasting time coordinating with parents across different time zones.
schedulingTutors.suggestedProduct: A tutor-first scheduling platform with recurring sessions, package management, parent portal for availability, and integrated payments—designed for 1–20 students, not hundreds.

recruitingAgencies.summary: Small agencies find enterprise ATS tools overkill and spreadsheets unsustainable. They need simple pipeline tracking and client-facing reports.
recruitingAgencies.topPainPoint: Paying for bloated ATS software with features they never use, while still tracking candidates in spreadsheets and losing great hires to slow processes.
recruitingAgencies.suggestedProduct: A lean ATS for agencies: candidate pipeline, client branding, simple reporting, and integrations with job boards—priced for teams under 50, not enterprise.

restaurantWaitlist.summary: Independent restaurants struggle with waitlist chaos: paper lists, phone calls, and no visibility into wait times for customers.
restaurantWaitlist.topPainPoint: Guests calling repeatedly to check wait times, hosts overwhelmed with manual list management, and no way for customers to get real-time updates without standing at the host stand.
restaurantWaitlist.suggestedProduct: A simple digital waitlist with SMS updates, real-time wait estimates, and a customer-facing status page—no tablets required, works from a single phone or tablet.

items.a: PainRadar is a SaaS idea discovery platform that analyzes online discussions to find recurring pain points, product opportunities, and SEO angles.

items.q: Where does the data come from?
items.a: We analyze discussions from forums, review sites, and community platforms to extract real complaints and frustrations people are expressing.

items.q: How accurate is the opportunity scoring?
items.a: Our scoring combines demand signals, urgency indicators, competition analysis, and monetization potential into a composite score. It's a directional guide, not a guarantee.
Yes. You can export analyses as JSON files or copy them to your clipboard. PDF export is coming soon.
Yes! The free plan gives you 3 analyses per month. No credit card required.

items.q: How is this different from regular market research?
items.a: PainRadar automates what would take hours of manual research. Instead of browsing Reddit threads, we surface patterns and quantify opportunities for you.

items.q: Will you add more data sources?
items.a: Yes. We're actively working on integrating more sources including Reddit API, G2, Capterra, and niche forums.

indieHackers.title: PainRadar for Indie Hackers
indieHackers.description: Stop guessing what to build. PainRadar surfaces real pain points from Reddit, forums, and reviews so you can validate ideas before writing a single line of code.
indieHackers.audience: Solo founders and indie hackers building in public
painPoints: Wasting months building ideas that nobody wants
Guessing at problems instead of validating with real data
Not knowing how to find or prioritize customer complaints
Lacking time to manually research dozens of communities
benefits: Discover validated ideas in minutes, not months
See which pain points have the highest demand and urgency
Get AI-summarized insights from real customer conversations
Focus your limited time on building, not researching

agencies.title: PainRadar for Agencies
agencies.description: Win more clients by proposing solutions that solve real problems. PainRadar gives your team data-backed insight into prospect pain points.
agencies.audience: Digital agencies, consultancies, and service businesses
painPoints: Proposing generic solutions that don't address specific client pain
Struggling to differentiate in crowded RFP responses
Lacking concrete data to back up recommendations
Spending hours on manual research for each prospect
benefits: Generate client-specific pain point reports in minutes
Support proposals with real customer complaints and demand signals
Identify underserved niches and expansion opportunities
Equip your team with research that closes deals

startups.title: PainRadar for Startups
startups.description: Validate your roadmap with evidence, not intuition. PainRadar analyzes thousands of conversations to surface the pain points that matter most.
painPoints: Building features based on HiPPO opinions instead of data
Unclear which customer complaints deserve roadmap priority
Competitive landscape feels opaque and hard to quantify
Limited time for deep customer research before each sprint
benefits: Prioritize features using real demand and urgency scores
Understand competitive positioning from actual customer feedback
Identify adjacent problems and expansion opportunities
Align the whole team around evidence-based roadmaps

manualResearch.title: Manual Reddit Research
manualResearch.description: Manually scrolling through Reddit, forums, and review sites to find pain points. Many founders start here — but it's time-consuming and hard to scale.
painRadar: Automated analysis across Reddit, forums, G2, Capterra, and niche communities
Structured pain point extraction with severity scoring and source attribution
Opportunity scoring across demand, urgency, competition, and monetization
Product ideas and SEO keywords generated from real customer language
Results in minutes, not hours or days
alternative: No consistent scoring or prioritization framework
Hours of research for each topic

googleTrends.title: Google Trends
googleTrends.description: Google Trends shows search volume over time. Useful for high-level interest, but it doesn't reveal pain points, complaints, or product opportunities.
painRadar: Real customer complaints and frustrations—not just search volume
Pain point extraction with sentiment and severity
Product ideas and differentiation angles from actual discussions
Competitor gap analysis from review sites and forums
Context: what people say when they're frustrated, not just what they search
alternative: Search volume trends only—no qualitative insight
No pain point or complaint data
No direct link to customer language

surveyTools.title: Survey Tools
surveyTools.description: Survey tools like Typeform or Google Forms let you ask people what they want. But surveys require an audience, leading questions, and often miss unsolicited frustration.
painRadar: Analyzes unsolicited feedback—what people say when nobody's asking
No audience required; works from public discussions
Unbiased: real complaints, not survey-leading answers
Instant insights; no need to design, send, or wait for surveys
Scales to any number of niches and topics
alternative: Requires building or buying an audience first
People answer what they think you want to hear
Weeks to design, send, and analyze
One survey per topic; hard to scale

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.dataCollectedContent: PainRadar collects information you provide when creating an account, including your name, email address, and password. We also automatically collect usage data such as your IP address, browser type, device information, and pages you visit within our service. When you use our analysis features, we store the topics you search for and the results generated. We use cookies and similar technologies to maintain your session and improve our service. Analytics data helps us understand how our product is used and identify areas for improvement.
privacy.howWeUseContent: We use your data to provide, maintain, and improve the PainRadar service. This includes delivering pain point analyses, saving your searches and results, and personalizing your experience. We may use your email to send important service updates, security notifications, and product communications. Analytics data helps us understand usage patterns and develop new features. We also use your information to detect and prevent fraud, enforce our terms, and comply with legal obligations.
privacy.dataSharingContent: We share data with trusted service providers who help us operate PainRadar, including hosting providers, analytics services, payment processors, and email delivery services. These providers are contractually bound to use your data only for the purposes we specify and to protect it appropriately. We do not sell your personal data to third parties. We may disclose your information when required by law, to protect our rights, or in connection with a merger or acquisition. We do not share your data with advertisers for targeted advertising.
privacy.dataRetentionContent: We retain your account data and saved analyses for as long as your account is active. If you delete your account, we will remove your personal data within 30 days, except where we are required to retain it for legal, regulatory, or legitimate business purposes. Backup copies may persist for a limited period before being permanently deleted. Usage and analytics data may be retained in aggregated or anonymized form for longer periods to improve our service. You may request early deletion of your data by contacting us.
privacy.yourRightsContent: If you are in the European Economic Area, you have rights under the GDPR including the right to access your data, request rectification of inaccurate data, request erasure, receive your data in a portable format, restrict processing, and object to processing. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority. If you are a California resident, you have rights under the CCPA including the right to know what personal information we collect, the right to delete your data, the right to opt out of the sale of your data (we do not sell data), and the right to non-discrimination for exercising your rights. To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at painradar.app@gmail.com.
privacy.cookiesContent: We use essential cookies that are necessary for PainRadar to function, including session management and authentication. We use analytics cookies to understand how visitors use our service and to improve our product. Preference cookies remember your settings such as language and theme. You can control cookies through your browser settings; however, disabling essential cookies may affect the functionality of our service. Third-party services we use, such as analytics providers, may set their own cookies subject to their respective privacy policies.
privacy.contactContent: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us at painradar.app@gmail.com.

about.pageSubtitle: We help founders discover real problems worth solving.
about.missionContent:
PainRadar was born from a simple observation: too many founders build products nobody wants. We believe the best SaaS ideas come from real customer pain — not guesswork, not assumptions, not copying competitors.

Our mission is to make pain point discovery accessible to every founder, indie hacker, and product team. By analyzing real discussions from forums, review sites, and communities, we surface the problems that matter most — so you can build with confidence.
about.howContent:
PainRadar scans thousands of online discussions to identify recurring complaints, frustrations, and unmet needs. Our AI extracts structured insights — pain points, product ideas, SEO keywords, and opportunity scores — and delivers them in a format you can act on immediately.

Whether you're validating your next SaaS idea, researching a new market, or looking for content angles, PainRadar gives you the data-driven foundation to make better decisions.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.acceptanceContent: By accessing or using PainRadar ("the Service") at painradar.com, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, you may not use the Service. You must be at least 18 years of age to use PainRadar. If you are using the Service on behalf of an organization, you represent that you have the authority to bind that organization to these terms. Your continued use of the Service constitutes your acceptance of any modifications to these terms.
terms.descriptionContent: PainRadar is a B2C SaaS idea discovery platform that analyzes online discussions to identify recurring pain points, product opportunities, and SEO angles. The Service helps founders and entrepreneurs validate SaaS ideas by extracting insights from forums, review sites, and community platforms. PainRadar provides pain point detection, opportunity scoring, AI-generated product ideas, and keyword suggestions. The Service is provided through our website at painradar.com and may be accessed via web browser or other authorized means. We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue any aspect of the Service at any time.
terms.accountsContent: To access certain features of PainRadar, you must create an account and provide accurate, current, and complete information. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your password and for all activities that occur under your account. You must promptly notify us at painradar.app@gmail.com of any unauthorized access or use of your account. We are not liable for any loss or damage arising from your failure to protect your account credentials. You may not share your account with others or use the Service for any purpose that violates these terms or applicable law.
terms.billingContent: PainRadar offers both free and paid subscription plans. Free plan users receive limited analyses per month. Paid plans are billed on a recurring monthly basis unless otherwise stated. By subscribing to a paid plan, you authorize us to charge your payment method on each billing cycle. We may change our pricing with reasonable notice; continued use after a price change constitutes acceptance. Refunds are provided at our discretion; generally, we do not offer refunds for partial billing periods. You may cancel your subscription at any time through your account settings; cancellation will take effect at the end of the current billing period.
terms.prohibitedContent: You may not use PainRadar to: scrape, harvest, or extract data beyond normal use; reverse engineer, decompile, or attempt to derive the source code of the Service; harass, abuse, or harm others; violate any applicable law or regulation; impersonate any person or entity; interfere with or disrupt the Service or servers; use the Service for any illegal or unauthorized purpose; or circumvent any access controls or usage limits. We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these prohibitions. Violations may also result in legal action.
terms.ipContent: PainRadar and its licensors own all right, title, and interest in the Service, including the software, design, content, trademarks, and other intellectual property. You retain ownership of any data or content you submit to the Service. By submitting content, you grant us a limited, non-exclusive license to use, process, and store that content as necessary to provide and improve the Service. You may not copy, modify, distribute, or create derivative works of the Service without our express written permission. The PainRadar name and logo are our trademarks and may not be used without authorization.
terms.disclaimersContent: THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED. WE DISCLAIM ALL WARRANTIES INCLUDING MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT. WE DO NOT GUARANTEE THAT THE SERVICE WILL BE UNINTERRUPTED, ERROR-FREE, OR SECURE. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, PAINRADAR AND ITS AFFILIATES SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. OUR TOTAL LIABILITY SHALL NOT EXCEED THE AMOUNT YOU PAID US IN THE TWELVE MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. We are not liable for events beyond our reasonable control, including force majeure.
terms.terminationContent: We may suspend or terminate your access to PainRadar at any time, with or without cause or notice. We may terminate your account immediately if you violate these terms or engage in conduct we deem harmful to the Service or other users. Upon termination, your right to use the Service ceases immediately. We may delete your account data and saved analyses within a reasonable period after termination. Provisions that by their nature should survive termination, including intellectual property, disclaimers, and limitation of liability, shall survive. You may terminate your account at any time through your account settings.
terms.changesContent: We reserve the right to modify these Terms of Service at any time. We will notify you of material changes by posting the updated terms on painradar.com and updating the "Last updated" date. For significant changes, we may also notify you by email to the address associated with your account. Your continued use of the Service after the effective date of changes constitutes your acceptance of the revised terms. If you do not agree to the modified terms, you must stop using the Service and may cancel your account. We encourage you to review these terms periodically.
terms.contactContent: If you have any questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us at painradar.app@gmail.com.
